name: Lot of US Definitive and Commemorative Stamps (1920s-1970s)
description: A lot of United States postage stamps on an album page fragment. Includes: a strip of five 2c red Washington (c. 1922-25, e.g., Scott #554); a strip of three 1c green Washington (c. 1922-25, e.g., Scott #552); a vertical and a horizontal pair of 3c purple Statue of Liberty (1954, Scott #1035); a single used 3c Statue of Liberty; a 3c blue Washington embossed envelope cut square (c. 1916, e.g., Scott #U436); a horizontal pair of 3c blue Jefferson (1938, Scott #807); a strip of three 10c green American Clock (1975, Scott #1616); and a pair of 5c Christmas (1973, Scott #1508). Most are unused, attached to the page.
justification: This is a collection of multiple distinct stamps sold together as a single lot.
verificationNotes: Lot contains a mix of common US definitive and commemorative stamps from the 1920s to 1970s.
Condition is mixed, with some items appearing unused (likely hinged) and others used.
The blue circular item is a cut square from a stamped envelope.
